front: What is the role of transdermal iontophoresis in peptide delivery?
back: Iontophoresis uses low electrical current (0.1-0.5 mA/cm2) to drive charged peptides across skin. Electroosmosis enhances neutral molecule transport. Enables controlled, non-invasive delivery of peptides (e.g., LHRH, insulin). Patches with electrodes and reservoirs.
